items.title: 'A succulent Chinese meal?': These are the 2026 Sounds of Australia

children.content: A variety of iconic Australian recordings are added to the Sounds of Australia collection, as part of an annual update by the National Film and Sound Archive.

gallery.altText: Screengrab of a grey-haired man being wrestled into a car by a few policeman

thumbnail.altText: Screengrab of a grey-haired man being wrestled into a car by a few policeman

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: ACT police, prosecutors accused of 'egregious conduct' over botched prosecution

children.content: Lawyers for a Canberra man cleared of 14 family violence charges are calling for an apology and compensation, after police found evidence the complainant fabricated some claims.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: 'Wholly and solely systemic racism': Anger over closure of Indigenous health hub

children.content: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

gallery.altText: A woman with glasses in front of an Indigenous artwork.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with glasses in front of an Indigenous artwork.

items.title: Teenagers brandish knives, hammer at Canberra shopping centre

children.content: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

image.altText: A police car with its lights on outside a shopping centre.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

image.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: Australia aids Artemis II as NASA astronauts return to the Moon

children.content: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

gallery.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.
Astronauts wearing orange suits inside a spacecraft.

thumbnail.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.
Astronauts wearing orange suits inside a spacecraft.

items.title: Iran's 'safe passage' signals a new global divide

children.content: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

image.altText: China President Xi Jinping.

items.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Kharg Island if Iran refuses deal to end war

children.content: As thousands more US troops arrive in the Middle East, Donald Trump threatens to wipe out Iran's energy plants and oil wells if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: Donald Trump standing in a doorway aboard Air Force One.

items.title: From Porepunkah to Thologolong: The long search for Dezi Freeman

children.content: When the parents of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart flew to Melbourne for a police service, little did they know their son's killer would emerge just days later.

articles.title: Police continue hunt for anyone who assisted fugitive Dezi Freeman

children.content: Police say they will come down hard on anyone who helped Dezi Freeman, who was shot dead by police yesterday after a massive seven-month manhunt.

image.altText: A screen grab of a man looking seriously at a the camera.

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

children.content: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry returning from France. Within minutes of trying to board he found out his identity had been stolen.

image.altText: A man dressed in large ears and a Pinoccio nose with a police hat.

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.

articles.title: It's $2.5m a dose and it's giving some children another chance at life

children.content: Children born with a rare neuromuscular disorder are being given another chance in life thanks to a new treatment experts are calling a "game-changer".

image.altText: A young child being held up by her father

gallery.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

thumbnail.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

items.title: How supermarket chicken could get cheaper because of grain breakthrough

image.altText: UQ Professor Ian Godwin stands in a field of sorghum.

children.content: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive feed grains in livestock systems that could mean cheaper meat and eggs.

items.title: Junior pay rates to go for over 18s, ASX opens flat

children.content: Young people older than 18 to earn the same as adults in landmark decision. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Five social media companies investigated over response to teen ban

children.content: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.

image.altText: A red-haired woman in a light-coloured blazer stands in a corridor.

items.title: How to tell if you have insufficient insurance for your home and contents

children.content: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

gallery.altText: Grandfather and grandson walking to a yellow weatherboard home across a green lawn

thumbnail.altText: Grandfather and grandson walking to a yellow weatherboard home across a green lawn

items.title: Moon mission quiz: How far does your lunar knowledge reach?

children.content: As NASA gets ready to launch humans around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years, buckle up and let's see if your space knowledge is up to our quiz.

image.altText: Image of half a Moon against a black background.

items.title: This luminous film shows a side of Iraq many Westerners have never seen

children.content: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

image.altText: People in a street holding up images and cut outs of Saddam Hussein.

items.title: Passengers fear further V/Line overcrowding as free travel begins

children.content: Commuters say they are worried overcrowding on V/Line train services could worsen as free travel begins for Victorians to ease the pain of the fuel crisis.

image.altText: The interior of a full train carriage showing full seats and the aisle full of standing passengers

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

children.content: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

image.altText: a woman stares at the camera with a houseboat in the background

items.title: The ABC rode along with paramedics to examine the NT service's under-resourcing

children.content: The ABC rode along with St John Ambulance NT paramedics to hear the impact under-resourcing is having on the workforce, including stories of staff being assaulted on the job.

items.title: 'You are just an immigrant': Millions in unpaid wages, modern slavery uncovered

children.content: Millions in stolen wages, threats of visa cancellation and modern slavery by Australian employers have been uncovered in the first year of a national program.

image.altText: a woman from colombia who now lives in australia smiles at the camera

items.title: Safety officers to patrol Hobart CBD to curb antisocial behaviour

children.content: A trio of council-employed public safety monitors will soon be patrolling "hot spots" in the Hobart CBD, to try to curb antisocial behaviour and theft.

image.altText: An empty shopping mall strip.

items.title: Wine manufacturer De Bortoli has its own solution for workers amidst housing crisis

children.content: There is plenty of work on offer in the western Riverina, but finding somewhere to live can be a challenge. The situation is forcing some employers to think outside the box.

gallery.altText: A man stands between rows of a vineyard.

thumbnail.altText: A man stands between rows of a vineyard.

items.title: Concerns about light and noise from $1b outback energy project resolved

children.content: The battery project, which will provide back-up power for up to 80,000 homes in Broken Hill, will go ahead with new conditions after a dispute was resolved between the company and an outback stargazing business.

image.altText: Aerial view of highway with a few residences pictured on the ground.

items.title: These states have introduced free public transport amid fuel crisis

children.content: As fuel prices continue to rise some states have moved to make public transport free, while others say that fares are already low enough. Find out where.

image.altText: Train crosses over bridge above water with view of Brisbane city in the background

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent

children.content: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

image.altText: Two members of the Italian men's football team pump fists and celebrate a goal, arms around each other

items.title: 'A complete wizard': 15yo sensation smacks another rapid half-century in IPL

children.content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes a 15-ball half-century as he leads Rajasthan Royals to an easy victory over Chennai Super Kings.

image.altText: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi looks away in a huddle with his India under-19 World Cup teammates.

items.title: Myanmar's military chief makes move to become next president

children.content: Myanmar's military chief has stepped down to seek the presidency in the general's latest efforts to maintain power behind the facade of a civilian administration.

image.altText: Commander-in-Chief of Myanmar's armed forces, Senior General Min Aung Hlaing

items.title: Banned Xerri reveals Essendon abuse leading to blood-wiping incident

children.content: Kangaroos ruckman Tristan Xerri gives evidence at the AFL tribunal as he accepts a suspension for rubbing blood from his nose on the face of Essendon captain Andrew McGrath.

image.altText: Tristan Xerri rubs blood on the face of Andrew McGrath

items.title: Massive Matildas hype hasn't translated to A-League Women fandom

children.content: Despite the recent hype of the Matildas, support for the A-League Women remains relatively low.
